lens: public good and social impact in Singapore

design_contract.mandate: Every idea must be built with a deliberately chosen visual direction and interaction model, and copy written for the target user — not for developers. Reading the ui field and rules/CONTENT_RULES.md is mandatory before writing a single line of code.

design_diversity_mandate.priority: HIGHEST — this overrides any default layout instinct
design_diversity_mandate.rule: Before picking an idea or writing any code, read rules/DESIGN_DIVERSITY.md completely. Complete the Design Diversity Audit: look at the last 5 built POCs, identify their dominant structural patterns, and choose an app_type from app_type_pool that has NOT appeared in any of those 5 builds. The chosen app_type must be structurally visible in the finished UI.
design_diversity_mandate.why: The last several POCs have been too visually similar — all styled web pages with creative data presentation but the same underlying rhythm. This mandate exists to force genuine experiential variety.
design_diversity_mandate.enforcement: If the chosen app_type conflicts with the idea's ui.interaction_model, the app_type wins. Adapt the interaction model to fit the app_type, not the other way around.

app_type_pool.description: Browser game with score, lives, win/lose states, stages — full viewport play area, no traditional page structure
UI rendered inside a visible phone/device frame — bottom nav bar, thumb-zone-aware layout, native app feel
Spatial canvas fills the viewport — zones, pins, overlays are the primary interface, no content below the fold
2D drag/match/sort/reveal educational experience with XP, streaks, badges, and celebration moments
Three.js, WebGL, or canvas animation is the primary surface — the moving thing IS the content
Looks like Reddit/Twitter/Instagram — scrollable feed with votes/reactions in a social product shell
Scroll-driven storytelling — sections animate in as the user scrolls, the page tells a story
Monospace font, CLI aesthetic, typed output, command-style inputs — dark background, text IS the UI
Conversational UI — message bubbles, assistant voice, back-and-forth thread, no forms
Full-screen, single-focus, oversized targets — one thing per screen, huge typography, no dense chrome
Data visualisation as art — generative, ambient, or aesthetic; the visual IS the data
Central node with branching radial navigation — content organised as a graph, not a list
Two or more panels that react to each other — drag divider, mirror state, the comparison IS the interface
Magazine layout with full-bleed images, pull quotes, reading rhythm — scrolling article structure
The UI breathes — glanceable, time-driven animation, live feel, minimal direct interaction

anti_patterns: CRITICAL: Do not build a new POC with the same app_type as any of the last 5 built POCs — check app_type_pool and rules/DESIGN_DIVERSITY.md
Do not build a card grid unless ui.interaction_model explicitly says card grid
Do not repeat the same visual direction as any other POC already in the repo
Do not use generic dashboard chrome with sidebar + header + card body
Do not expose builder language (POC, component, scaffold, slice, demo, prototype, seed data, simulation) in the UI copy
Do not use forms as the primary UI unless ui.interaction_model says form-led
Do not skip installing suggested libraries — they exist to make the concept distinctive
Do not add components, sections, or buttons the target user would not want or need
Do not front-load all features — show only what the user needs for their current step
Do not write copy for a developer audience — write for the target user
required_before_build: FIRST: Read rules/DESIGN_DIVERSITY.md and complete the Design Diversity Audit
FIRST: Identify the last 5 built POCs and their dominant structural patterns
FIRST: Choose an app_type from app_type_pool that has NOT appeared in those 5 builds
Read the idea's ui.direction and understand what it means visually
Read ui.interaction_model and plan the layout around it — but override it if it conflicts with your chosen app_type
Read ui.suggested_libraries and install them before writing UI code
Read ui.distinctive_feature — this is the one thing that must feel crafted
Read ui.avoid — never do these things for this concept
Read rules/CONTENT_RULES.md completely before writing any copy
Answer: what does this app do, for whom, in one plain-language sentence? Put that on the first screen.
Answer: what is the single first action the target user should take? Make that the most prominent element on the first screen.
The first screen must communicate the concept's point of view within 5 seconds to a member of the target audience
content_rules_summary: Every word in the UI must be written for the target user, not for a developer
Banned words in user-facing copy: POC, prototype, demo, seed data, simulation, persona, component, scaffold, implementation, concept, generated, placeholder, mock, fake, test
Every component must pass: would the target user, right now, want this? Remove anything that fails
The first screen above the fold must contain: what this does (one line), who it is for (implicit), and the first action only
Empty states must prompt the next action, not explain the technology

ideas.title: Food Donation Route
ideas.summary: Plan efficient pickup and drop-off routes for small rescue runs.
ideas.why_fast: The interesting challenge is visualising a multi-stop rescue route as an animated SVG path the user can interactively reorder.
ideas.prototype_goal: Simulate the core value of Food Donation Route in-browser with believable local data and zero backend dependencies.
ideas.auth_strategy: No authentication. Use one-click persona buttons such as login as resident, customer, volunteer, caregiver, or store owner depending on the flow.

storage_strategy.secondary: IndexedDB only if the POC needs larger offline caches, many records, or image/file blobs
storage_strategy.server_database: not allowed for the initial POC

ideas.simulation_mode: Use seeded demo records, optimistic UI updates, and fake notifications or status changes so the end-to-end workflow is testable by one person.
demo_seed_data: 3 to 8 sample places records tailored to Singapore context
2 to 3 personas with prefilled preferences, saved items, or history
at least 1 edge case record to demonstrate empty states, stale data, or exceptions
ideas.success_signal: A new user can switch into a persona, complete the main workflow in under 2 minutes, and understand the concept without any manual setup.
non_goals: real identity verification or account systems
production-grade multi-user synchronization
complex backend integrations before concept validation
build_notes: Prefer deterministic mock data over external APIs.
Keep state resettable so demos can be rerun quickly.
Treat notifications, queues, and matching as simulations unless a real API is trivial.

ui.interaction_model: animated route diagram — a vertical timeline showing pickup stops in order, with an SVG animated path connecting them; user can drag to reorder stops or toggle which stops to include
ui.distinctive_feature: animated dotted SVG path connecting stops in sequence; reordering a stop redraws the path with a smooth animation
ui.avoid: standard list of addresses; static map thumbnail; form-first without route visualization
ui.experience_goal: user feels like they are planning a physical journey, seeing the route take shape as they add stops

ui.interaction_model: step intake form with live status tracker — a 4-step form for submitting a device; a status tracker sidebar showing the donation pipeline stage; a thank-you confirmation with a reference number

ui.avoid: single long form without stages; no feedback after submission; list of accepted devices only
ui.experience_goal: donor feels their contribution is being tracked and will reach someone who needs it

ui.avoid: small text; multiple items visible at once; search-first interface; anything requiring precise tapping
ui.experience_goal: a 70-year-old with no smartphone experience can follow along without help

ideas.title: Medication Reminder
ideas.summary: Send discreet reminders with optional caregiver visibility.
ideas.why_fast: The interesting challenge is representing a day's medication schedule as a circular clock-face SVG rather than a list of reminders.
ideas.prototype_goal: Simulate the core value of Medication Reminder in-browser with believable local data and zero backend dependencies.
ideas.auth_strategy: No authentication. Use one-click persona buttons such as login as resident, customer, volunteer, caregiver, or store owner depending on the flow.

storage_strategy.secondary: IndexedDB only if the POC needs larger offline caches, many records, or image/file blobs
storage_strategy.server_database: not allowed for the initial POC

ideas.simulation_mode: Use seeded demo records, optimistic UI updates, and fake notifications or status changes so the end-to-end workflow is testable by one person.

2 to 3 personas with prefilled preferences, saved items, or history
at least 1 edge case record to demonstrate empty states, stale data, or exceptions
ideas.success_signal: A new user can switch into a persona, complete the main workflow in under 2 minutes, and understand the concept without any manual setup.
non_goals: real identity verification or account systems
production-grade multi-user synchronization
complex backend integrations before concept validation
build_notes: Prefer deterministic mock data over external APIs.
Keep state resettable so demos can be rerun quickly.
Treat notifications, queues, and matching as simulations unless a real API is trivial.

ui.interaction_model: clock-face timeline — a circular 24-hour clock face as the primary view showing medication times as arcs; tapping a time slot reveals the medication detail and a large confirm button
ui.distinctive_feature: circular 24-hour clock rendered in SVG with medication times as colored arcs; the current time hand sweeps in real time
ui.avoid: list of reminders with checkboxes; notification dashboard; anything that looks like a todo app
ui.experience_goal: user feels calm and in control; the clock face makes the day's schedule immediately visible

build_intent: simulation-first concept validation for Singapore public-good apps

build_defaults.auth: No real auth. Persona switching only.
build_defaults.persistence: Prefer localStorage for user-created state and seeded simulations.
build_defaults.database_policy: Use IndexedDB only when localStorage becomes too limited for record size, quantity, or offline assets.
build_defaults.backend_policy: Avoid a server backend for the first pass unless the concept cannot be demonstrated without it.
build_defaults.simulation_policy: Workflows should be demoable by one person using seeded Singapore-specific data and fake state transitions.
build_defaults.sharing_policy: Export, copy-link, or local JSON import/export is acceptable; real accounts and cross-device sync are out of scope.
build_defaults.public_api_policy: Public APIs from data.gov.sg are allowed when they materially improve the concept. Use axios helpers plus SWR for in-memory stale-while-revalidate caching, dedupe requests across components, and choose conservative refresh intervals instead of aggressive polling.
build_defaults.public_api_refresh_guidance: Prefer per-feed refresh intervals matched to the data type. Avoid sub-minute polling unless the endpoint truly requires it, and fall back gracefully when a cached response is temporarily stale.

reference_guidance: Treat each idea as a POC, not a production MVP.
Assume the builder will reference this JSON directly when scaffolding the app.
Default to browser-only state, seeded data, and simulation-heavy flows.
If a multi-sided workflow exists, expose simple buttons to switch personas instead of building auth.
Use IndexedDB only if the POC genuinely needs larger offline storage than localStorage can comfortably handle.
When a concept uses a public API, prefer axios for fetch helpers and SWR for in-memory caching, deduping, and stale-while-revalidate behaviour.
Do not fetch public APIs more often than necessary; tune refresh intervals to the feed rather than polling every few seconds.
